text:
A host of competitors from last year’sOlympic Gameshave reportedly returned their medals after complaining they were rusty and flaking. The medals were designed by LVMH and produced by La Monnaie, an enterprise that has minted coins and medals in France since the ninth century.

Over 1,000 medals were handed out at the Paris Olympics, which included 329 events across 32 sports.

USA and China both claimed 40 gold medals, with Japan clinching 20. USA won the most medals in total after also bagging 44 silvers and 42 bronzes.

According toThe Times, signs of deterioration began to appear just days after athletes had received their medals. And more than 100 winners have since returned their shiny prizes.

Faulty varnish has been blamed for the issues, with bronze medals affected more than the golds and silvers. The bronze medals are mainly made from copper but include a small amount of zinc and tin.

And the damage appears to have occurred after the mint opted to use an anti-corrosion varnish to comply with EU regulations when the usage of the toxic chromium trioxide was banned.

The French mint plans to replace the medals and are working with Paris 2024 chiefs to assess claims and understand the reasons behind the damage.

Read more:Caitlin Clark says she was thankful after missing out on the Olympics



Some competitors have taken to social media to air their grievances over the state of the medals, with French swimmer Clement Secchi describing his peeling medal as “crocodile hide”.

Meanwhile, his team-mate in the 4x100m relayYohann Ndoye-Brouardjested that his prize had the appearance of a 1924 medal.

Great Britain won a total of 65 medals at the Paris Games after ending the competition with 14 golds, 22 silvers and 29 bronzes.

And following the Games, Paris 2024 president Tony Estanguet said: “There are always things that could be improved and we’ve had issues day-in day-out to resolve.

Don't miss:Usain Bolt explains why he snubbed Olympic Games and refused TV appearances

Don't miss:British Olympic hopefuls 'left homeless' and forced to abandon LA 2028 plans

Don't miss:Prince William explains the real reason he missed the Paris 2024 Olympics

“But if someone told me on the eve of the opening ceremony of how things would go, I would have said, ‘sign me up’. It’s wonderful, it’s a huge success, a collective success, incredibly so.

We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

“France has rallied together to pull off this edition of the Olympic Games. I’m so proud of the success story. I have no regrets at all.”

Some of the medals from the Rio Games in 2016 and Tokyo Games in 2021 also had a number of issues, with Rio spokesman Mario Andrada previously explaining: “The most common issue was that they were dropped or mishandled, causing the varnish to come off and leading to rusting or blackening where they were hit.”

title: Rusty Paris Olympic medals recalled as French embarrassed after athletes complain | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Tom Parsons
description: Olympic medals from the Paris Games have been blemished with rust.
